examples.title: Bootstrap Loader Algorithm
examples.description: Simplified representation of how bootstrap loader works
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Bootstrap_Loader
BEGIN
  INPUT: None (executed from ROM)
  OUTPUT: Control transferred to OS kernel
  
  1. POWER_ON
  2. CPU_SET_PC_TO_ROM_ADDRESS
  3. EXECUTE_POST
     IF post_failed THEN
        DISPLAY_ERROR
        HALT
     ENDIF
  4. BIOS_INITIALIZE
  5. BOOT_DEVICE <- READ_BOOT_ORDER_FROM_CMOS
  6. READ_BOOT_SECTOR FROM BOOT_DEVICE
  7. LOAD_SECTOR_INTO_MEMORY(0x7C00)
  8. JUMP_TO(0x7C00)
  9. OS_LOADER_EXECUTES
END
examples.explanation: The bootstrap loader is the first program that runs when a computer is powered on. It is stored in ROM and is responsible for initializing the system and loading the operating system kernel into memory.

examples.title: Simplified Boot Sector Reading
examples.description: Demonstrates how the boot sector is read during boot process
examples.code:
def read_boot_sector(device_path):
    '''
    Simulates reading boot sector from a storage device
    Boot sector is typically 512 bytes
    '''
    SECTOR_SIZE = 512
    
    # Open device in binary read mode
    with open(device_path, 'rb') as device:
        # Read first sector (boot sector)
        boot_sector = device.read(SECTOR_SIZE)
    
    # Check boot signature (0x55AA at end)
    boot_signature = boot_sector[-2:]
    if boot_signature == b'\x55\xaa':
        print("Valid boot sector found")
        return boot_sector
    else:
        print("Invalid boot sector")
        return None

def load_os_from_boot_sector(boot_sector):
    '''
    Loads OS from boot sector code
    '''
    # Jump to boot code location (typically 0x7C00)
    # In real systems, this transfers control to OS loader
    print("Transferring control to OS loader...")
    return True

# Example usage
# boot_sector = read_boot_sector('/dev/sda')
# if boot_sector:
#     load_os_from_boot_sector(boot_sector))
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates how a boot sector is read from a storage device. In real systems, this is performed by the BIOS/UEFI firmware and the bootstrap loader.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - constant time for reading fixed sector size

examples.title: Simple Device Driver Structure
examples.description: Demonstrates basic structure of a device driver in C
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates the basic structure of a device driver. Device drivers are crucial in OS Generation as they provide the interface between the operating system and hardware devices.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of drivers
